./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1939:** ^The sqlite3_initialize() routine initializes the
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1940:** SQLite library.  ^The sqlite3_shutdown() routine
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1941:** deallocates any resources that were allocated by sqlite3_initialize().
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1946:** A call to sqlite3_initialize() is an "effective" call if it is
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1947:** the first time sqlite3_initialize() is invoked during the lifetime of
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1948:** the process, or if it is the first time sqlite3_initialize() is invoked
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1949:** following a call to sqlite3_shutdown().  ^(Only an effective call
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1950:** of sqlite3_initialize() does any initialization.  All other calls
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1953:** A call to sqlite3_shutdown() is an "effective" call if it is the first
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1954:** call to sqlite3_shutdown() since the last sqlite3_initialize().  ^(Only
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1955:** an effective call to sqlite3_shutdown() does any deinitialization.
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1956:** All other valid calls to sqlite3_shutdown() are harmless no-ops.)^
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1958:** The sqlite3_initialize() interface is threadsafe, but sqlite3_shutdown()
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1959:** is not.  The sqlite3_shutdown() interface must only be called from a
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1962:** sqlite3_shutdown().
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1964:** Among other things, ^sqlite3_initialize() will invoke
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1965:** sqlite3_os_init().  Similarly, ^sqlite3_shutdown()
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1966:** will invoke sqlite3_os_end().
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1968:** ^The sqlite3_initialize() routine returns [SQLITE_OK] on success.
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1969:** ^If for some reason, sqlite3_initialize() is unable to initialize
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1973:** ^The sqlite3_initialize() routine is called internally by many other
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1975:** invoke sqlite3_initialize() directly.  For example, [sqlite3_open()]
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1976:** calls sqlite3_initialize() so the SQLite library will be automatically
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1977:** initialized when [sqlite3_open()] is called if it has not been initialized
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1979:** compile-time option, then the automatic calls to sqlite3_initialize()
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1980:** are omitted and the application must call sqlite3_initialize() directly
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1982:** it is recommended that applications always invoke sqlite3_initialize()
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1988:** The sqlite3_os_init() routine does operating-system specific
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1989:** initialization of the SQLite library.  The sqlite3_os_end()
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1990:** routine undoes the effect of sqlite3_os_init().  Typical tasks
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1993:** setting up a default [sqlite3_vfs] module, or setting up
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1994:** a default configuration using [sqlite3_config()].
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:1996:** The application should never invoke either sqlite3_os_init()

./node_modules/better-sqlite3/deps/sqlite3/sqlite3.c:36388:** In deployment, sqlite3FaultSim() *always* return SQLITE_OK (0).  The

./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:46:Though not required, [it is generally important to set the WAL pragma for performance reasons](https://github.com/WiseLibs/better-sqlite3/blob/master/docs/performance.md).
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:55:import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:60:## Why should I use this instead of [node-sqlite3](https://github.com/mapbox/node-sqlite3)?
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:62:- `node-sqlite3` uses asynchronous APIs for tasks that are either CPU-bound or serialized. That's not only bad design, but it wastes tons of resources. It also causes [mutex thrashing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resource_contention) which has devastating effects on performance.
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:63:- `node-sqlite3` exposes low-level (C language) memory management functions. `better-sqlite3` does it the JavaScript way, allowing the garbage collector to worry about memory management.
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:64:- `better-sqlite3` is simpler to use, and it provides nice utilities for some operations that are very difficult or impossible in `node-sqlite3`.
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:65:- `better-sqlite3` is much faster than `node-sqlite3` in most cases, and just as fast in all other cases.
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:69:In most cases, if you're attempting something that cannot be reasonably accomplished with `better-sqlite3`, it probably cannot be reasonably accomplished with SQLite in general. For example, if you're executing queries that take one second to complete, and you expect to have many concurrent users executing those queries, no amount of asynchronicity will save you from SQLite's serialized nature. Fortunately, SQLite is very *very* fast. With proper indexing, we've been able to achieve upward of 2000 queries per second with 5-way-joins in a 60 GB database, where each query was handling 5–50 kilobytes of real data.
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:71:If you have a performance problem, the most likely causes are inefficient queries, improper indexing, or a lack of [WAL mode](./docs/performance.md)—not `better-sqlite3` itself. However, there are some cases where `better-sqlite3` could be inappropriate:
./node_modules/better-sqlite3/README.md:81:Upgrading your `better-sqlite3` dependency can potentially introduce breaking changes, either in the `better-sqlite3` API (if you upgrade to a new [major version](https://semver.org/)), or between your existing database(s) and the underlying version of SQLite. Before upgrading, review:
